key_departures.impact: Jones carried a $14.55M cap hit and was set to be released to save $7.75M. Was RB1A alongside Jordan Mason in 2025, though production dipped. Leaves RB depth thin behind Mason.
Cap casualty — release saves $10.9M. Generated 31 pressures and 4 sacks in 2025 but didn't justify $15M cap number. Depletes IDL depth alongside Allen's departure.

Expected release due to cap constraints. Finished 2025 with just 1 sack and 14 pressures across 17 games — second-worst PFF grade among Vikings defenders with 249+ snaps. Dead cap hit of $17.53M (or $12.66M with post-June 1 designation). Along with Hargrave, creates a significant IDL void.

Retired after suffering three concussions in 2025 alone (six documented in career). Was the starting center; retirement opens $8.4M in cap space but leaves a critical hole at C. Michael Jurgens (7th-round, 2024) and Blake Brandel are the internal candidates.

Signed 3yr/$35M ($23M guaranteed) with Raiders. Was the WR3 behind Jefferson and Addison, but served as primary slot/flex option. Departure creates meaningful WR depth need. Vikings won't pay $11.7M/yr for a WR3.
Signed 4yr/$14M ($8M guaranteed) with Saints. 4-year starter at P for Minnesota. Departure forces Vikings to find a new punter via FA or draft.

Contract expired; widely expected to retire after an emotional Week 18 sendoff. Six-time Pro Bowler and 14-year Viking. Team posted a career highlights video suggesting retirement. If confirmed, creates a major starting S void — no true replacement currently on roster beyond Josh Metellus and Jay Ward.
Two-time Pro Bowl FB officially retired. Leaves no fullback on roster. Vikings may move away from the FB position altogether under O'Connell's scheme, but special teams impact is a real loss.

signings.analysis: Wilson had a breakout 2025, posting the NFL's third-best pressure rate among LBs (15.3%) after stepping in when Blake Cashman was injured. Re-signed on a 3yr/$22.5M deal with $12.5M fully guaranteed — a meaningful investment. At $7.5M AAV, this is mid-to-upper starter tier for LB. The $12.5M fully guaranteed signals this is their guy. Alongside Cashman and the tendered Ivan Pace Jr., Minnesota's LB room is now addressed. Age (turns 32 in September) means drafting depth is still prudent, but the starting need is resolved.

Pierre follows new secondary coach Gerald Alexander from Pittsburgh to Minnesota. At $4.25M AAV with only $3.7M guaranteed, this is a clear depth/CB3 signing — not a starter investment. Vikings enter FA with only Byron Murphy Jr. and Isaiah Rodgers as established CBs. Pierre adds familiarity with the new DB coach and brings quality special teams value, but the CB need is not resolved. Vikings still need a CB2/CB3 of consequence, likely through the draft.

Exclusive rights tender locks Redmond in for 2026 at the minimum ($1.075M). Was arguably Minnesota's best interior DL in 2025 (~75% of defensive snaps), coming from the UFL. Retaining him is essential, but at minimum salary it's not a market investment. With both Hargrave and Allen gone, Redmond becomes the de facto IDL starter, but the depth chart is dangerously thin. IDL remains a critical need — a multi-year extension for Redmond is expected but not yet done. Need only minimally addressed by this tender.

Lowest-level RFA tender. Pace is a rotational LB and core special teamer. If another team submits an offer sheet, Vikings can match. Since Pace was undrafted, Vikings receive no compensation if they don't match. This is a hold move to keep depth — contributes to the LB group but doesn't change any need tier.

ERFA tender at minimum. Richter carved out a special teams role in 2025. Developmental EDGE behind Greenard, Dallas Turner, and Andrew Van Ginkel. Does not address any EDGE need — strictly a depth roster hold.
ERFA tender at minimum salary. Practice squad-level depth RB. Does not address the RB need left by Aaron Jones' departure. Jordan Mason remains the clear RB1; Zavier Scott is a camp body.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: No QB has been signed as of March 10. Vikings are heavy favorites to sign Kyler Murray (expected minimum deal once officially released by ARI on March 11) but it is NOT confirmed. J.J. McCarthy (injury-prone, 10 starts in 2 seasons) remains the only QB under contract. QB is still a Tier 1 critical need until a veteran is officially signed.

Ryan Kelly retired after three concussions in 2025, leaving zero established starting centers on the roster. Michael Jurgens (7th-round, 2024) and Blake Brandel are the only internal options. No free agent center has been signed. This escalates from a moderate need to a critical Tier 1 need. Must be addressed in free agency or early draft rounds.

Jonathan Allen (expected release) and Javon Hargrave (release) both leave the roster, wiping out two starting DTs. Jalen Redmond (ERFA tender, minimum salary) is now the de facto starter but has only one year of NFL starting experience and is on a prove-it deal. IDL escalates to Tier 1 — depth is critically thin with no meaningful free agent additions made yet.

Harrison Smith is widely expected to retire (contract expired, career highlights video posted, emotional Week 18 sendoff). No official retirement announcement as of March 10, but all signs point to departure. Josh Metellus and Jay Ward are the only options, neither of whom is a proven starter at the level Smith operated. Safety escalates to Tier 1 critical need.

Aaron Jones' release leaves Jordan Mason as the clear RB1 with Zavier Scott (minimum tender) as the only other RB under contract. C.J. Ham also retired, eliminating FB depth. No meaningful RB additions in FA. With Mason as a viable starter but no proven backup, RB moves from moderate to a Tier 2 need — should target a complement via mid-round draft pick or cheap FA.

Jalen Nailor departed to Las Vegas on 3yr/$35M. Jefferson and Addison remain as WR1/WR2, but the WR3 slot is now empty and no replacement has been signed. Vikings need a reliable slot/flex option, escalating this from a depth need to a genuine Tier 2 priority.

James Pierre (2yr/$8.5M, $3.7M guaranteed) adds CB3 depth and special teams familiarity with new DB coach Gerald Alexander, but is not a starter-caliber investment at $4.25M AAV. Vikings still have only Murphy Jr. and Rodgers as established starters. CB remains a Tier 2 need — Pierre is a rotational body, not the answer to the CB2/3 competition need.

Greenard (28, $19M AAV) remains on roster but is actively being shopped for a trade as he seeks a new deal. Trade has not been completed as of March 10. If Greenard stays, EDGE is adequately staffed with him, Dallas Turner, Andrew Van Ginkel, and Bo Richter. If Greenard is traded, EDGE immediately becomes Tier 1 critical. Holding at Tier 2 given unresolved trade status — monitor closely.

Eric Wilson re-signed on 3yr/$22.5M ($12.5M fully guaranteed) — a real investment. Cashman and Ivan Pace Jr. also return. Wilson's breakout 2025 (3rd-best pressure rate among LBs) and the meaningful guarantee structure confirms this is their guy. LB drops from Tier 2 to Tier 3 — depth addition via late draft round is fine, but no longer a pressing need.
Ryan Wright signed with New Orleans (4yr/$14M). Vikings have no punter under contract. Must address via free agency or draft — a Tier 2 operational need that must be resolved before the season.

key_additions.headline: Breakout linebacker (NFL's 3rd-best QB pressure rate in 2025) locked up on meaningful multi-year deal — LB need resolved
Familiar face to new DB coach Gerald Alexander fills CB3 depth role at below-starter cost
Vikings' best interior DL in 2025 retained on minimum tender; multi-year extension expected but not yet done
Rotational LB and special teams contributor retained on lowest-level RFA tender

key_losses.headline: Cap casualty — veteran RB1A released despite key leadership role, leaves Jordan Mason as sole proven RB
One-year DT experiment ends; release alongside Allen wipes out both starting interior DL spots
Former Pro Bowler cut after dismal 2025 (1 sack, worst PFF grade among Vikings starters), adding dead cap pain
Four-time Pro Bowl center retires after 3 concussions in 2025; creates critical void at C with no proven replacement
WR3 departs for $11.7M/yr — Minnesota won't replace that output cheaply, leaving a genuine slot depth void
4-year starting punter leaves for New Orleans; Vikings must find a replacement before the season

key_losses.contract_summary: Contract expired — retirement expected (not yet official)
key_losses.headline: Six-time Pro Bowl safety's career appears over after emotional Week 18 sendoff; creates major hole at S with no clear successor
Two-time Pro Bowl fullback and 10-year Viking retires; team may phase out position under O'Connell

notes: All signings confirmed via multiple credible sources (ESPN, NFL Network, Star Tribune, CBS Minnesota) through March 9–10, 2026. Jonathan Allen and Aaron Jones releases were reported as 'expected at start of league year (March 11)' — not yet official as of March 10 but treated as confirmed given Schefter/Fowler reporting. Harrison Smith retirement is strongly implied but not officially announced. Kyler Murray signing is NOT included — he cannot be officially signed until released by ARI on March 11; Vikings are heavy favorites (-295 on DraftKings) but no deal is confirmed. Jonathan Greenard trade discussions are ongoing; he remains on the Vikings roster as of March 10 — included as a departure risk flag only. Cap figures sourced from OverTheCap, Spotrac, and ESPN/NFL Network reporting.
